mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer income, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137846.0 as of Sept 2013 reflects ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 2.61% represents accommodative monetary policy relative to historical norms. Persistence of FEDFUNDS below 3.0% signals continued monetary support, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of months where FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening consumer and residential demand. This joint expansion signals robust aggregate demand growth, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 381416.0 and HOUST at 1065.0 as of Aug 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint demand acceleration months produce returns not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 111.91 reflects post-crisis capacity utilization recovery. Sustained month-over-month INDPRO growth above 0.2% signals strengthening supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, supporting profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth months do not produce significantly higher 5-day returns than negative growth months (t-test p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 5.1% represents elevated slack relative to pre-crisis levels. A rise in UNRATE above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 212.571 remains subdued. When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth falls below 0.1%, it signals deflationary pressure that reduces real discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or low-inflation months produce returns not significantly higher than high-inflation months (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11675.7 billion chained 2017 dollars reflects post-crisis recovery momentum. When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, it signals strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or high-growth quarters produce 60-day returns not significantly higher than low-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move in opposite directions during labor market transitions. When PAYEMS accelerates above 150,000 persons month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously declines, this joint signal indicates strong labor market momentum that drives consumer confidence and equity valuations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 150,000 AND UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137846.0 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of Sept 2013
regime_hint: labor market acceleration with synchronized employment and unemployment improvement
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint labor market acceleration months produce returns not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.12).
